task: I’m trying to get a genuinely rigorous picture of the current market for AI and machine learning postdoc roles, because I want a shortlist I could actually use for applications instead of a shallow search result dump. Please start in CryptPad Sheets and create a spreadsheet named AI Postdoc Opportunities with columns for university or lab, department or institute or PI, exact position title, research area, deadline or start date if shown, posting link, and a short note saying how you verified it. Then use Google to build a coverage list of roughly the top 30 U.S. computer science schools along with major AI labs and related institutes, and work through that list carefully using official university job boards, department hiring pages, institute sites, lab pages, faculty group pages, and central hiring portals. As you find anything promising, open the actual posting page in its own tab and read enough of it to confirm it is really a current postdoctoral research opening in AI, machine learning, or a closely related area, not a faculty search, PhD opening, internship, or industry research scientist role. If you need a second official page like a lab hiring page or institute announcement to confirm it, open that too and leave both tabs available so I can inspect the evidence myself. For schools or labs where nothing turns up, please do a second pass across multiple official sources so the absence is at least reasonably verified. As you go, fill the spreadsheet with every verified opening and make sure each row corresponds to a posting tab you still have open. Near the end, keep the spreadsheet visible along with several of the posting tabs and any supporting hiring pages, because I want visual proof of the search trail, and then add a short summary of the main patterns you noticed and which opportunities look strongest or most relevant.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent created a CryptPad spreadsheet and successfully renamed it to 'AI Postdoc Opportunities'. While the initial attempt to paste headers via clipboard failed due to browser restrictions, the agent eventually populated the sheet with a comprehensive list of institutions, research areas, and verification notes, satisfying the requirement for the spreadsheet's existence and structure.

R2: success - The agent compiled a broad list of top U.S. CS schools (e.g., MIT, Stanford, CMU, UC Berkeley, UIUC, Cornell, UW, Georgia Tech, Princeton, Caltech, UCLA, UCSD, UMich, UT Austin, Columbia, Harvard, Yale, Penn, Maryland, Wisconsin-Madison, Purdue, Duke, Johns Hopkins, Brown, Rice, UNC, USC, UMass Amherst, NYU, Northeastern) and major AI labs (Microsoft Research, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, Allen Institute for AI, Toyota Research Institute, SRI, Brookhaven, Los Alamos, Argonne, Oak Ridge, Lawrence Berkeley). This is clearly visible in the final spreadsheet content.

R3: success - The agent performed a systematic search across the checklist using subagents to query official university job boards, department hiring pages, institute sites, lab pages, and faculty group pages. The action history shows extensive web searching and fetching from official domains (e.g., .edu, .gov, .mil, .microsoft.com, .metacareers.com).

R4: success - The agent verified each opening by fetching the actual posting pages (e.g., Interfolio, Workday, university career portals). The subagent results confirm that the agent filtered out faculty searches, PhD openings, internships, and industry research scientist roles, focusing specifically on postdoctoral research positions.

R5: success - The final spreadsheet contains rows for verified openings with columns for university/lab, department/PI, position title, research area, deadline/start date, posting link, and verification note. The data is populated and corresponds to the findings from the search process.

R6: success - The agent's subagent reports explicitly mention checking multiple official sources for institutions and noting where no current openings were found (e.g., Allen Institute for AI, specific labs at Princeton). This demonstrates a second-pass verification for institutions with no identified openings.

R7: success - The final spreadsheet is visible in the screenshots, and the agent included a summary section at the bottom of the spreadsheet (rows 28-32) detailing patterns and recommendations based on the search.

rubric_results.verification: Grader can see an open spreadsheet with the exact title and visible headers for university/lab, department/institute/PI, position title, research area, deadline/start date, posting link, and verification note.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent created a CryptPad spreadsheet and successfully renamed it to 'AI Postdoc Opportunities'. While the initial attempt to paste headers via clipboard failed due to browser restrictions, the agent eventually populated the sheet with a comprehensive list of institutions, research areas, and verification notes, satisfying the requirement for the spreadsheet's existence and structure.

This is clearly visible in the final spreadsheet content.

rubric_results.requirement: The search is carried out systematically across the checklist using official departmental, institute, lab, faculty, and university hiring sources.
rubric_results.verification: Browser history/tabs show repeated navigation from Google to official university or lab domains for multiple institutions, demonstrating a broad audit rather than a few isolated searches.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent performed a systematic search across the checklist using subagents to query official university job boards, department hiring pages, institute sites, lab pages, and faculty group pages. The action history shows extensive web searching and fetching from official domains (e.g., .edu, .gov, .mil, .microsoft.com, .metacareers.com).

rubric_results.requirement: Each included opportunity is verified on the actual posting page as a current postdoctoral research position relevant to AI/ML, with non-postdoc or irrelevant roles excluded.

The subagent results confirm that the agent filtered out faculty searches, PhD openings, internships, and industry research scientist roles, focusing specifically on postdoctoral research positions.

rubric_results.requirement: Every verified opening is entered in the spreadsheet with all requested fields completed as available, including a direct link and a verification note.
rubric_results.verification: Spreadsheet rows contain populated fields for each verified role, and the posting links correspond to the open tabs used for verification.

rubric_results.requirement: Institutions with no identified openings receive an additional verification pass using multiple official sources to support the absence conclusion.
